events.title: Floor 41 elevators stuck for 6 hours, Rita brought a folding chair
events.description: Around 9 AM the Floor 41 elevator banks froze between floors. Maintenance took six hours to clear. By hour two there was a small impromptu salon in the lobby. Rita Cheng had a folding chair.

events.title: Council sets election date, urges every resident to vote
events.description: The Triboro Council fixed a date for the long-delayed election and launched a get-out-the-vote push. Both factions welcomed the announcement with suspicious warmth.

events.title: Kirwin Foods puts Mood Muffins in every floor drop this week
events.description: Kirwin's announced Mood Muffins would ship in every floor's supply drop through the week. A Seed Club points bonus applies.

events.title: Aurora Report: Green for the eleventh straight day
events.description: The daily Aurora Report held at Green for an eleventh consecutive day. The Aurora Cult gave thanks; meteorologists urged calm.

events.title: League of Trades reports 'minor' pressure anomaly on Floors 38-41
events.description: The League acknowledged a water-pressure anomaly across Floors 38-41, called it minor, and blamed 'possible sabotage.' Residents on those floors report worse.

events.title: Theater District's official troupes demand crackdown on pirated performances
events.description: The sponsored repertory companies signed an open letter calling for action against unlicensed performers reading from novels and newspapers they don't own.

events.title: Aurora Cult announces 'Divine Dice' night to boost attendance
events.description: With attendance flagging, the Aurora Cult announced another Divine Dice night, pitched as a way for wagers to win the Aurora's favor.

events.title: Floor 41 elevator bank down again; impromptu lobby salon forms
events.description: The Floor 41 elevators froze for the third time this month. By the second hour a small social scene had formed in the lobby.

events.title: Child on Floor 52 says she saw 'a boy in the Aurora'; parents told not to worry
events.description: A girl on Floor 52 told neighbors she saw a boy's face in the smog outside a sealed window. The Health Committee advised parents it was almost certainly nothing.
